content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates some AoA fluency — body-based inquiry (turn 4, 10), tracking the packaging/defense pattern (turn 8), and gently pointing at voice-as-care via 'what if the critic is scared of the tenderness?' (turn 14). However, several signature moves are notably missing or misapplied. Turn 6's 'what if the critic is actually right?' is closer to a Byron Katie reframe than an AoA move and risks the trap of arguing the critic's content — the client even names this. The signature questions that fit precisely here — 'what are you defending?' and 'what's the case against it?' — are never deployed. The war-itself-as-the-problem frame is not named. VIEW is never applied to the voice. The 'voice as care' reframe arrives as a clever insight in question form rather than as a quiet offering, which the client immediately pattern-matches to IFS. Mix of appropriate hits and missed/inappropriate deployments lands this at 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Generic opener; not a signature question but a reasonable orient."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Feel-over-figure move: body-based inquiry deployed appropriately when client is in figuring mode."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'What if the critic is actually right?' is a Byron Katie-flavored move, not an AoA signature; client correctly names it as a trap that feeds the critic. Misses 'what are you defending?' which would fit here."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Naming the packaging/defense pattern is consistent with AoA tracking; redirects to feeling rather than concept."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Stay there. Don't explain it. Don't name it.' — solid feel-over-figure invitation at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Tracks the exact moment the voice interrupted; appropriate sequencing."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Voice-as-care reframe attempted but delivered as a clever insight question; client immediately maps to IFS. The AoA twist (you are not your parts; meet with okay) is absent."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["voice-as-care"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["whats-the-case-against-it", "what-are-you-defending", "apply-view-to-the-voice", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "view", "resistance", "rehearsing-how-to-say-it"],
  "notes": "Coach has AoA instincts (body, feel-over-figure, tracking the defense) but misses the specific signature questions and the central frame — the war is the problem, not the voice. Turn 6 risks the trap of validating/arguing the critic's content."
}
